tabInstructions.general: Edit the core project data, lifecycle status and description. Link the organization's applicants and the project contacts (focal points and project managers) below. Changes save automatically.
tabInstructions.overview: Complete all required fields. These form the foundation of your annual report.
tabInstructions.surveys: Use this section to self-assess your project's performance across four areas: data quality, ecosystem and collaboration, data uptake and use, and the contribution of pooled funding. For each question, select a rating and explain your choice clearly and concisely, using evidence from the reporting period. Keep responses short (2-3 sentences) and focused on what changed, improved, or was enabled. Avoid lengthy narrative or descriptions of activities alone.
tabInstructions.risk: Identify and assess risks to your project. For each risk, evaluate its likelihood and impact, then describe how you plan to mitigate it.
tabInstructions.achievements: Highlight up to three key achievements from this reporting period. For each, briefly describe what was achieved, explain its significance to the project's goals and target population, and include links to any supporting evidence or outputs. Keep entries concise and evidence-based.
tabInstructions.partnerships: List the organizations that contributed as implementing, counterpart, or ecosystem partners to the project. For each, describe what improved or changed because of the partnership, and provide links to supporting evidence.
tabInstructions.results: Document cases where your project's data informed real decisions and produced measurable outcomes. Add at least 3 results. For each, describe the context and decision gap, explain how your data drove a concrete decision, and describe the resulting impact with quantitative evidence where available.
tabInstructions.lessons: Capture up to five lessons from this reporting period that shaped or will shape project implementation. For each lesson, select a category, describe what your organization learned, and explain what you changed or plan to change as a result.
tabInstructions.external-coverage: Document how your project's work reached external audiences and stakeholders during this reporting period. For each item, select the type of coverage or engagement, describe what was shared, indicate the reach or uptake, and provide links to supporting materials.
tabInstructions.workplan: Review the planned timeline (baseline) for each activity and check off the quarters reflecting the updated timeline for this reporting period. For each activity, select a progress update and add a short comment justifying the status where relevant.
tabInstructions.expenditure: Enter the actual expenditure for this reporting year against each budget category, in USD. Approved budgets, differences, sub-totals, indirect support costs and totals are calculated automatically. Add a comment where the expenditure differs meaningfully from the approved budget.
tabInstructions.indicators: Report progress against each indicator for this reporting period. For each indicator, enter the value achieved this year, select whether it is on track, and add a short comment explaining the result. Baselines and targets were set when the report was prepared and are shown for reference.
tabInstructions.transfers: List the organizations you transferred funds to during this reporting period. For each organization, provide its name, website and type, then enter the amount transferred this year (USD) and link it to the workplan activity it supported. Amounts for previous years are shown for reference; only the currently selected year is editable.
tabInstructions.complementary: List the contributors that provided complementary funding to the project during this reporting period. For each contributor, provide its name, website and funding type (in cash or in kind), then enter the total contribution this year (USD) and link it to the workplan activities it supported. Amounts for previous years are shown for reference; only the currently selected year is editable.
tabInstructions.narratives: Enter the written narrative texts that make up the project document. Each field captures one narrative section from the proposal. Changes save automatically when you leave a field.

questions.label: Alignment with and Commitment to CRAF'd Principles
CRAF'd Data Ecosystem Impact & Use Cases

authorization.heading: Authorization
messages: We grant the Complex Risk Analytics Fund (CRAF'd) a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free, perpetual license to use, reproduce, adapt, distribute, and publicly display the submitted quotes, photographs, videos, and other communication materials solely for communications, outreach, advocacy, and reporting purposes.

We confirm that we hold all necessary rights and have obtained required consents from any individuals or third parties featured, in line with applicable laws and ethical standards. Ownership remains with the originating party. Where feasible, CRAF'd will provide attribution as specified, except where impracticable or inconsistent with the format or purpose of use. Materials shall not be used in a misleading or defamatory manner.

remarks.achievement: Briefly describe a major result or milestone achieved while implementing the project during this reporting period. For example: The project developed a cloud system that now delivers up-to-date weather and impact feeds.
remarks.significance: Explain why this achievement matters, how it contributed to project objectives, and what changed as a result. For example: The cloud system enables drought outlooks from 2 to 4 months in advance and flood alerts up to 16 days ahead. Using this data, local NGOs started targeted seasonal funding allocations ahead of the hazard impact.
remarks.links: Provide direct links to reports, tools, dashboards, articles, or other materials that demonstrate or document the achievement.

testimonials.leadershipInstruction: Use this sub-section to provide one quote from your organization's leadership reflecting on the project's results and the contribution of CRAF'd support. Limit the quote to a maximum of 25 words. Also, submit a high-quality photo of the person quoted here and label them accordingly.
testimonials.partnerInstruction: Use this sub-section to provide up to three quotes from partners or users of the project's data or insights. Quotes should describe how partners contributed to the project or how they used the data or insights in practice, particularly for crisis anticipation, prevention, or response. Limit each quote to a maximum of 20 words. Provide properly labelled photos of the person quoted here if available.

placeholders.photoLabel: How to label the person in the photo

remarks.type: Select the type of coverage and engagement.
remarks.description: Provide the title and a short description of the engagement or coverage, explaining what was shared or referenced and in what context. For example: Presented at an international data forum to share the project's data collection methodology and gather feedback.
remarks.reachIndicator: Provide a quantitative indication of reach or uptake, such as number of participants, downloads, views, citations, or mentions, where available. For example: Received feedback from over 50 workshop participants.
remarks.links: Include direct links to publications, recordings, dashboards, or other materials documenting the engagement or coverage.

remarks.category: Select the category of the lesson learned.
remarks.lessonLearned: Briefly describe what your organization learned while implementing the project during this reporting period. For example: The project learned that data downloads alone did not provide insight into who was using the platform or how the data informed decision-making.
remarks.adjustmentInformed: Explain what you changed (or will change) in project implementation or reporting as a result of this lesson. For example: An API user registration mechanism was introduced to capture user profiles and usage patterns, enabling better analysis of data uptake and use.

remarks.context: Briefly describe the problem, risk, or decision gap in a fragile or crisis-affected setting. For example: Repeated intercommunal tensions in Akobo County, South Sudan, were causing cyclical displacement, with limited early warning to prevent escalation before violence occurred.
remarks.dataDrivenDecision: Explain and, where possible, quantify how the project's data or insights informed concrete decisions and actions, including their relevance for resource allocation and programming. For example: Project's data was integrated into the displacement prediction model to forecast population movements up to 3 months ahead. In May 2024, elevated risk forecasts triggered a targeted programmatic response, including inter-community dialogues engaging over 200 local leaders, youth, and officials.
remarks.resultingImpact: Describe the difference this made in practice, including quantitative results where available. For example: Preventive action contributed to 2,800 fewer displaced people than neighboring areas, achieved a ROI of over 6.6× emergency funding, and informed early-action models now used in 6 countries.
remarks.links: Provide full links to relevant reports, articles, and other materials that demonstrate the use case and impact.

remarks.partnerOrganization: List the organization that contributed as an implementing, counterpart, or ecosystem partner to the project.
remarks.result: Briefly describe what improved or changed because of the partnership, including concrete outputs or outcomes (e.g. better data, wider access, shared standards). For example: The organization collaborated with two additional data partners to deliver multi-sectoral needs assessments; during the 2026 humanitarian planning cycle, the partners aligned their data collection approaches and developed a standardized implementation package that will be integrated into their API in future iterations, improving consistency and reuse of data.
remarks.links: Provide direct links to reports, datasets, dashboards, tools, or publications that demonstrate the results of the partnership.

remarks.achievedValue: Enter the value achieved for this indicator during the reporting year.
remarks.status: Select whether the indicator is on track, off track, or ahead of schedule relative to its target.
remarks.comment: Briefly explain the result, any deviation from the target, and contributing factors.
